title: Gritty Gooey Thai Rice Khao Pad
description: A bitter, pillowy northern Thai rice dish cooked in a clay pot that balances the umami of shio-koji with the richness of coconut milk for a solo-dinner delight.
instructions: Rinse 1.5 cups jasmine rice until water runs clear.
In a clay pot, combine rinsed rice, 200ml coconut milk, and 200ml water; bring to a gentle simmer.
Cover and cook for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until rice is tender and liquid absorbed.
While rice cooks, mince 2 garlic cloves, 1 tsp ginger, and slice 1/4 onion.
In a small bowl, whisk together 20g shio-koji, 20ml soy sauce, 1 tsp tamarind paste, and 1 tbsp lime juice.
When rice is done, turn heat to low and stir in the garlic, ginger, onion, and the shio-koji mixture.
Drizzle 1 tbsp vegetable oil and 1 tsp sesame oil over the rice and toss to combine.
Sprinkle 2 sliced scallions and 1 tsp chili flakes, then stir until fragrant.
Taste and adjust with additional salt if needed, though shio-koji provides sufficient saltiness.
Remove from heat, let rest 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
Serve the gritty, gooey Thai rice warm, optionally accompanied by fresh cucumber slices.
